description: Query vehicle ECUs for standardized Parameter IDs across OBD-II service modes, decoding raw byte responses into typed values with physical units and caching PID support per vehicle

actors.name: Diagnostic System
actors.description: The application building and dispatching PID query commands to the vehicle

support_discovery: At connection time, query PID support listing commands (mode 1, PIDs 0x00/0x20/0x40/0x60/0x80/0xA0/0xC0) to build the vehicle's supported PID set
Cache the supported PID set for the lifetime of the connection; do not re-query on each command
Mode 6 (monitor data) is only valid on CAN protocols; flag it as unsupported on non-CAN connections
query_execution: Set the ECU target header on the adapter before each query when targeting a specific ECU
Append an optional frame-count digit to PID commands for early response termination when the adapter supports it
Flush the receive buffer before sending each command to discard stale data
Append a carriage return to every command byte sequence before transmission
Read the response until the adapter prompt character is received or the timeout expires
response_parsing: Strip null bytes and normalize line endings from raw adapter output
Split response into frames; each frame identifies its source ECU via the header
Filter frames by ECU target bitmask to exclude responses from unintended ECUs
Constrain data bytes to the expected length for the PID; discard extra bytes
Pass constrained bytes to the PID-specific decoder function
return_values: All numeric sensor values must be returned with their physical unit (RPM, km/h, °C, %, g/s, kPa, V, etc.)
A null/empty response must be distinguishable from a zero-value response
If the decoder raises an error, return a null response rather than propagating the exception
unsupported_handling: Do not transmit a PID command if the vehicle has not advertised support for it, unless force_query is true
Return a null response immediately for unsupported PIDs

given: vehicle connection is not in vehicle_connected state
not_connected.result: Returns a null response immediately without transmitting any command

given: requested PID is not in the vehicle's advertised support set
pid_not_supported.result: Returns a null response; no command is transmitted to the vehicle

given: PID is supported or force_query is true
adapter sends the command but no ECU response arrives before timeout
no_response.result: Returns a null response; caller may retry the query

given: no frame matches the target ECU filter
no_matching_ecu.result: Returns a null response; the targeted ECU did not reply

given: a response is received from the target ECU
the decoder cannot interpret the response bytes
decode_error.result: Returns a null response; raw bytes are logged for diagnostics

given: PID is supported or force_query is true
at least one response frame matches the ECU target
decoder produces a valid typed value
successful_query.result: Returns decoded value with physical unit; caller receives a non-null response object

errors.message: No active vehicle connection. Connect before querying.
This vehicle does not support the requested parameter.
The vehicle did not respond to the query. Check the connection and retry.
No response from the targeted ECU. It may not be present in this vehicle.
The vehicle returned data that could not be decoded for this parameter.

events.name: obd.pid.response
events.description: A PID was queried and a decoded value was received from the ECU

events.name: obd.pid.unsupported
events.description: A query was skipped because the vehicle does not advertise support for the PID

related.reason: Active vehicle_connected state must exist before PID queries are possible
Real-time sensor readings use this PID query infrastructure
DTC read (mode 3) and clear (mode 4) are implemented as PID-layer commands
VIN reads via mode 9 PID query

goals.description: Query vehicle ECUs for standardized Parameter IDs across OBD-II service modes, decoding raw byte responses into typed values with physical units and caching PID support per vehicle

success_metrics.measurement: Successful operations divided by total attempts
Errors that auto-recover without manual intervention

constraints.description: Must degrade gracefully when dependencies are unavailable

tradeoffs.reason: integration failures can cascade across systems
